en_body:
TITLE (Vietnamese): CONCEPT I-4
DATE: 12/09/2007

Part Four: Self-Creation
 "You believe me, don't you, Thạch?"
 For the first time his brother called him "em" with such tenderness! Thạch softened beyond imagination. He looked at his brother, his eyes again weak and full of admiration as when he was a child always begging to follow him out to play, gathering duckweed to feed fighting fish, fighting fish, catching crickets, fighting crickets, then playing foosball and shooting billiards, practicing the games of "grown men." But pain again twisted something within Thạch, making him restless beyond measure. His whole beautiful dream was shaken by jealousy. The first affection he had believed fitted perfectly into all his ideal choices and silent waiting for one time of love. Now was the hour of truth.
 "Nhạn and I decided to part the last time she came to visit me. The reason for the ending seemed simple yet also very profound. I answered Nhạn absolutely through the poem I made for her as a birthday gift.
 :One dot is excess upon the page,
 The fresh white page itself excess.
 But without the page there would be absence
 With page, no dot, it cannot be done.
 Yet the space between the two lines above
 Yet the space between the two lines below
 Yet the space between the between above
 And then the space between between the nothings:
 Ending a continuous line uncontinued
 Oh, that is the miracle of rise and go
 Love, oh, that is the beauty of returning to lie down
 To find the needless dot makes wholeness again.
 Done but not ended, becoming needful
 Needful yet excess, this passage below!
 Therefore one must know where to place the dot
 (In truth, a dot anywhere is all the same)
 The same, returning to a single dot: *
Brother Tung continued: "There are regions of the world that are unreal because they are yet are not. Those who live in that illusory world, they are unreal like shadows of ghosts. They are not real because they let themselves drift deep into a world no longer real. Their strange, monstrous points enchant eyes that still cherish uncanny beauties. But they do not 'live,' only appear, therefore they cannot endure. They cannot stop at any point of time. They drift forever and can hardly rest. I do not blame Nhạn, or you, but I want you to know that room is Nhạn's only weapon against stepping into a new reality. Nhạn wants to live forever in that storybook of hers, and to be the leading character, a tragic actress, in a drama written, directed, and performed by the framework of Nhạn's consciousness. That room is like a film for which Nhạn chooses the audience. I trusted you too greatly and never thought that you were not only my younger brother. Therefore thought itself, the meticulous analysis of life, is truly too excessive and wasteful, because it gives us no attention for the Reality we are living and that is worth living. I chose Nhạn because I idealized life too greatly...thinking only I could save her from the hell Nhạn obstinately colors gray over life. It turns out within me remains one weakness, the Messiah Complex.** Only now I understand Reality is what one creates within oneself. And I slapped Nhạc."
 One month later, Thạch and Nhạn were married. Tung did not attend his younger brother's wedding. Nhạn laughed very much beside Nhạc, and throughout all the years living with Thạch in a house where she negotiated for a room of her own to rewrite her memoir and continue her diary upon the walls, Nhạn never again spoke Tung's name.
 The day when Thạch carried the four-legged folding ladder from the car into Nhạn's room, he, breaking his custom, began allowing his feeling and intellect to gather again the final words brother Tung had spoken to him: "It's perculiar for a man to change of his own freewill into a work of art. All the results of poetic strivings lie so far from its author that one can hardly believe that it is a man-made creation. In the case of an actor, though, the reverse is true. The actor is himself his own creation, his own work of art." ****
 And now sitting here, Thạch remembered the single thing Nhạn had said to Thạch about Tung since the two were preparing to marry: "Tung himself is the volcano while we are only the rows of trees!"
.......................................................................................................................
*"Not An Ellipsis" (Hoàng-Ân, Dallas, TX, 1987)
**"Messiah Complex": the compulsion to save
***"It is very rare to find a man who by his own mind transforms himself into a work of art. All creations born from the maker's striving lie so far from the artist that one can hardly think those creations were made by the human mind (but seem as grace from the Source of Becoming inspired them). In the case of an actor, the reverse is true. The actor himself is his own creation, his artistic work is himself self-born-self-made within the process of creation." (Quoted from the film "The Sacrifice)

CONCEPT I-4
note: The closing quotation is attributed in the source to Andrei Tarkovsky's film The Sacrifice and frames the actor as his own created artwork.
